steps.title: Identify the cost structure
steps.content: The first book costs $16. Each book after the first costs $11. If $x$ is the total number of books purchased, then $(x - 1)$ books are purchased at the $11 rate.

steps.title: Build the equation
steps.content:
Total cost = cost of first book + cost of remaining books:
$$y = 16 + 11(x - 1) = 11(x - 1) + 16$$

steps.title: Verify with a test value
steps.content: For $x = 3$: $y = 11(2) + 16 = 22 + 16 = 38$. Check: first book $16 + two more at $11 each = $16 + $22 = $38$. Correct.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$y = x + 16$': Charges $1 per additional book instead of $11. ✗
  • Choice B — '$y = 11x + 16$': Charges $11 for all $x$ books including the first, then adds $16 on top. For $x = 1$: $y = 27$, but should be $16. ✗
  • Choice C — '$y = 11(x - 1) + 16$': $16 for the first book and $11 for each of the $(x - 1)$ additional books. For $x = 1$: $y = 16$. For $x = 2$: $y = 27$. ✓
  • Choice D — '$y = (x - 1) + 16$': Charges $1 per additional book instead of $11. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
